llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Only one AI arm exists (human_ai). This is compared against the human-only baseline (human_human) where participants collaborated with another human.
llm_extracted.notes: The primary statistic reports '50% more ads per worker, per unit time' for human_ai vs human_human, which is a clear +50% increase in productivity (throughput). The regression coefficient 1.954 (SE 0.197) is also provided, but the paper does not explicitly state whether the outcome was log-transformed or in levels. Given the magnitude of the coefficient (~1.95) and the reported 50% increase, it is plausible the outcome was log-transformed (since exp(1.954) ≈ 7.05, which doesn't match 1.50), but this is not explicitly confirmed. The 50% increase is the clearest effect measure. Additional rows report communication and delegation effects within each arm type, but these are not the main human_ai vs human_human comparison.
source_quotes: Participants in human-AI teams submitted 50% more ads per worker, per unit time than their counterparts in human-human teams, with results consistent when demographic controls are added (Table 2).
